title: Constellation branding system — marks, naming, icon rules, and a brand-review methodology
summary: Grounds decision #2191 (six forks) and the #2192 design-AI reviewer training card. The 2026-07-03 cross-repo branding review (rendered gallery plateau:branding.html, generated from live assets) inventoried every mark, favicon, icon, theme token, and name spelling across web-everything, frontierui, and plateau-app. Findings: the mark construction system is already coherent (40x40 squircle rx12, 45-degree gradient, white glyph, one hue per project) but six live choices are unratified — canonical prose spelling (Frontier UI 179 vs FrontierUI 34), WE favicon legibility at 16px (the 60%-opacity ghost-E), FUI mark strategy (weakest mark; letterforms vs three symbol-first candidates), Plateau violet drift (favicon #6d5efc vs DTCG token #6453f4), Plateau mark geometry (dual mesa vs two refinements), and the brand-review methodology itself. The methodology survey covers the practitioner frameworks — attribute/personality-driven rubrics (the design-system voice-and-tone pattern; Aaker's brand-personality dimensions as ancestry), Jungian brand archetypes, and uncodified taste — and lands on attribute-driven as the default: 3-4 attributes + one target feeling per project (WE: universal/principled/inevitable, calm trust; FUI: pioneering/pragmatic/kinetic, capable momentum; Plateau: elevated/steady/commanding, assured overview) unified by a landscape narrative (bedrock -> horizon -> plateau), because checklist-scoreable rubrics are the only shape an AI judge can be regression-tested against. Icon-set prior art (Lucide/Feather stroke+grid lint, Material Symbols keylines) grounds the check:branding gate. Full inventory + stats in reports/2026-07-03-constellation-branding-system.md; visual candidates at plateau:branding.html#proposals.
